comment.body:
veta is vega decay.
long veta = dealer is longer vega by the time until it reaches zero exposure buying back less and less vega,
short veta = opposite.

why? dealers hedge vol exposure too, but not in every timeframe, and not always... its the effect that matters.
vega needs 4 weeks or more in terms of maturity to have an effect on the dealer flow.
but look...
vega can be hedged only by options, bcs futures have no vega..
but it is vanna that matters the most from vega as its first derivative.
